# Situational Awareness LP — 13F Analysis

**Source:** SEC EDGAR, CIK 0002045724
**Manager:** Situational Awareness LP (San Francisco, CA; Delaware LP;
CRD #000333011; SEC file 801-132039)
**Filings analyzed:** 5 quarterly 13F-HR filings, Q4 2024 → Q4 2025
**Generated by:** `python -m wofo.thirteenf.cli analyze`

---

## Headline numbers

| Period | Positions | Reported Value | Top-5 share | Top-10 share | HHI | Effective N |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2024-12-31 |  6 |   $254.8M | 97.0% | 100.0% | 0.231 | 4.3 |
| 2025-03-31 | 12 | $1,005.6M | 76.1% |  95.7% | 0.243 | 4.1 |
| 2025-06-30 |  9 | $2,123.0M | 81.7% | 100.0% | 0.168 | 6.0 |
| 2025-09-30 | 25 | $4,138.4M | 66.4% |  84.7% | 0.123 | 8.1 |
| 2025-12-31 | 25 | $5,516.8M | 68.3% |  89.7% | 0.118 | 8.5 |

**Reported value** is the EDGAR-reported aggregate value. The 13F reports
long positions only.

## What the trajectory shows

1. **AUM scaled ~22× in four quarters**, from $255M to $5.5B reported.
2. **Concentration is loosening as the book grows** — the effective number
   of bets (1 / HHI) has roughly doubled (4.3 → 8.5), but the manager
   still runs a high-conviction book (top-5 ~70%).
3. **Position count flat-lined at 25** in the last two quarters even as
   AUM kept growing — capital is being added to existing names rather
   than diversifying further.
4. **Q4 2025 had zero "trim" or "hold-flat" positions among carry-overs
   from Q3** — every single retained name was added to. That is a
   directional signal of increased conviction across the entire book.

## Notes / known limitations

1. **Bloom Energy / Lumentum / Cipher Mining "round-trips" are not
   true exits.** In Q3 2025 the positions were held via different
   CUSIPs (ending in `-AH0`, `-AA4`, `-AD1`) which correspond to
   convertible / structured paper. In Q4 2025 the manager moved into
   the common-stock CUSIPs (`-107`, `-106`, `-109`). The economic
   exposure rolled forward — only the security identifier changed.
   This is a research insight (a deliberate cap-structure rotation)
   and an analyzer limitation we should fix by adding an
   issuer-based aggregation alongside the CUSIP-based one.
2. **13F is long-only.** Short positions, swap exposure, cash, and
   non-13F-reportable securities (most non-US, most options on
   non-13F names) are not visible.
3. **13F is stale by ~45 days.** The Q4 2025 filing was made
   2026-02-11. Any signal derived from it is mispriced by the time
   the public sees it.
4. **Reported value ≠ AUM.** The "tableValueTotal" is the aggregate
   reported value of long 13F-eligible positions on the report date.
   The fund's true AUM may be larger (cash, non-13F holdings) or
   smaller (gross vs. net of leverage).

## Reproducing this report

```bash
export WOFO_SEC_UA="Wooden Family Office contact@yourdomain.com"
python -m wofo.thirteenf.cli pull --cik 0002045724
python -m wofo.thirteenf.cli analyze
```

Outputs land in `wofo/data/13f/processed/`:
- `panel.json` — full panel keyed by `period|cusip`.
- `qoq_changes.json` — per-position quarter-over-quarter classification.
- `concentration.json` — per-period concentration metrics.
- One `<quarter>.json` per filing with parsed metadata + holdings.
